source_pr.title: cpuprofile: fix unstable test
source_pr.body:
Signed-off-by: crazycs520 <crazycs520@gmail.com>

<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #32080
close #32304

- [ ] No code

### Release note

<!-- bugfix or new feature needs a release note -->

```release-note
None
```

root_cause_explanation: The CPU-profile test relied on collecting enough profiling samples within a short window. Under CI load, the original profiling duration (hundreds of milliseconds) could be insufficient, leading to intermittent failures when parsing/validating the profile output. This PR stabilizes the test by increasing the sampling duration/timeout (to give the profiler time to produce data) and hardening the profiler's consumer broadcast path to avoid panics when consumers are modified concurrently.
fix_pattern: Avoid too-short sampling windows in profiling tests: increase the profile collection duration/timeout and make the producer/consumer path resilient to concurrency (e.g., guard or recover around consumer sends).
